items.title: Senescent cells cluster CTCF on nuclear speckles to instruct an alternative splicing program
items.author: Argyris Papantonis
items.content: Palikyras and colleagues investigate chromatin reorganization upon senescence induction, reporting that components of nuclear speckles coordinate chromatin rewiring and a senescence-associated splicing program. Interventions countering this reorganization delay senescence entry.

items.title: Plasma proteome profiling identified biomarkers for the differential diagnosis and molecular staging of neurodegenerative dementias
items.author: Marta del Campo
items.content: A large multicenter proteomics study identified blood biomarkers that distinguish Alzheimer’s disease, dementia with Lewy bodies and frontotemporal dementia, supporting biomarker-based differential diagnosis of neurodegenerative diseases.

items.title: Positioning TERT at the apex of aging
items.author: Ronald A. DePinho
items.content: This Perspective reviews TERT’s canonical telomere-maintenance and noncanonical regulatory roles in aging and outlines an evidence-based framework for evaluating TERT- and telomere-directed geroprotective strategies.

items.title: Disruption of CDK4/6–RARα–NF-κB axis attenuates senescence-associated inflammation and improves function during aging and following chemotherapy
items.author: Marco Demaria
items.content: Wang et al. report that the cell cycle regulators CDK4/6 also regulate the inflammatory phenotype of senescent cells, via modulation of retinoic acid signaling and NF-κB. Pharmacologically targeting this pathway reduces inflammation and improves physical function in models of chemotherapy-induced toxicity and aging.

items.title: Trained immunity links hematopoietic stem cell aging to aging-associated inflammation
items.author: Danica Chen
items.content: Mu, Barthez, Feng, Chen, Wang and colleagues report that SIRT3 suppresses the hematopoietic stem cell response to aging that drives maladaptive trained immunity, chronic inflammation and tissue functional decline in mice via inflammatory myeloid cells.

items.title: Spatiotemporal dynamics of tau extent and load increase in Alzheimer’s disease across four longitudinal cohorts
items.author: Pedro Rosa-Neto
items.content: Macedo et al. report the longitudinal spatiotemporal dynamics of tau from positron emission tomography. They find distinct dynamics in tau extent and load across Alzheimer’s disease stages, highlighting spatial extent metrics as sensitive indicators for early pathology, while load metrics correlate with advanced disease severity.

items.title: Spatial mapping and senolytic targeting of senescent and disease-associated microglia in aged mouse brain white matter
items.author: Marissa J. Schafer
items.content: Carver and colleagues profile the aged mouse brain using spatial transcriptomic and imaging analysis and identify a senescence- and disease-associated microglial-linked state enriched in white matter and reduced by senotherapeutic interventions.

items.title: Mapping and manipulating aged and senescent microglia
items.content: Single-cell spatial mapping and orthogonal approaches reveal a senescent and disease-associated microglial state that accumulates in the white matter of the aged mouse brain and is suppressed by senotherapeutic interventions.
